prompt:
Take the FIRST provided reference image (approved step 21 — dual-state illustration of placing the blood-filled vial into the white protector tube body, then placing the black DIALED cap on top) and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY in every element — same dual-state composition (LEFT: hand lowering blood-filled vial into white protector tube body; ARROW; RIGHT: hand placing black cap onto assembled tube), same hand poses, same vial appearance (red blood, ribbed cap), same tube proportions, same arrow, same line-art style on pure white background, same uniform medium-weight black ink line work.

MANDATORY MODIFICATION — fix the embossed text on the protector tube so it reads correctly from the viewer's straight-on angle. The current rendering has the text appearing reversed/mirrored, so reading top-down on the assembled tube comes out as 'HEALTH DIALED' or with letters scrambled. It MUST read 'DIALED HEALTH' top-down on the assembled vertical tube, with both words cleanly legible from our viewing angle.

DO NOT SWAP which word is on which section — DIALED stays on the BLACK CAP (top section), HEALTH stays on the WHITE BODY (bottom section). What changes is the TEXT ORIENTATION on each section so each word reads correctly from the viewer's angle and together they spell DIALED HEALTH from top to bottom of the upright assembled tube.

SPECIFICALLY:

  • BLACK CAP (top section of the assembled tube in the RIGHT state, and the cap held by the hand in the RIGHT state): the word 'DIALED' is embossed/printed on its side wall, rendered in BOLD SANS-SERIF CAPITAL LETTERS in WHITE (reversed-out of the solid black cap fill). The letters are oriented so the word reads LEFT-TO-RIGHT cleanly from the viewer's straight-on side view — D-I-A-L-E-D, no letters appearing mirrored, no letters appearing upside-down or backwards. The letters sit on the front-facing surface of the cap, properly aligned. The word should be the most prominent text on the cap and clearly spell DIALED to anyone looking at the image.

  • WHITE BODY (bottom section in both LEFT and RIGHT states): the word 'HEALTH' is embossed/printed on its side wall, rendered in BOLD SANS-SERIF CAPITAL LETTERS in BLACK LINE-ART (outlined letters or solid black letters against the white body). The letters are oriented so the word reads LEFT-TO-RIGHT cleanly from the viewer's straight-on side view — H-E-A-L-T-H, no mirroring, no upside-down letters. Properly aligned on the front-facing surface of the body.

  • Both words sit in the same horizontal orientation as each other (both reading left-to-right normally). Both must be the correct spelling when read from our viewing angle. No reversed letters, no scrambled order, no mirror writing.

  • When the assembled tube is upright (cap on top, body below), the eye scans TOP-TO-BOTTOM: first the BLACK CAP showing the word DIALED, then the WHITE BODY showing the word HEALTH. The combined top-down reading is: DIALED → HEALTH = 'DIALED HEALTH' (the brand name).

Do NOT modify anything else — same dual-state composition, same hands, same vial, same blood, same ribbed vial cap, same tube proportions (stubby pill-bottle, height ≈ 1.5x diameter, body slightly taller than cap), same arrow, same line weight, same white background, same line-art aesthetic, same overall framing. The ONLY change is the text orientation on the protector tube so both words read correctly left-to-right from the viewer's angle, with DIALED on the top cap and HEALTH on the bottom body, reading DIALED HEALTH top-down.

No frame, no border, no other labels, no numbers.
refImages: DIaled Health Instruction Graphics/Product Refs/protector tube top.png
